seoTitle: Connect Shopify to QuickBooks Online for Automated Sales
metaDescription: Sync Shopify orders, taxes, and payouts into QuickBooks Online automatically instead of manually tracking e-commerce sales.
intro: Manually copying hundreds of Shopify orders into your books doesn't scale. This guide shows how to connect Shopify to QuickBooks Online so daily sales, taxes, and payouts sync automatically into your ledger.

steps.heading: Find the Shopify connector
steps.body: Go to Commerce or Apps in the sidebar and search for Shopify by Intuit.

steps.heading: Start the connection
steps.body: Click Get App Now to begin the platform security connection process.

steps.heading: Authorize your store
steps.body: Enter your Shopify store URL and login credentials, then confirm read-write permissions to sync the databases.

steps.heading: Choose your sync style
steps.body: Select Consolidated Daily Summary to roll up order data and reduce ledger clutter, or choose individual itemized tracking.

steps.heading: Map accounts and fees
steps.body: Link Shopify sales, sales tax, and discounts to your QuickBooks accounts, set your checking account for payouts, and assign a category for processing fees.

faq.question: Should I use consolidated or itemized sync?
faq.answer: Consolidated Daily Summary rolls up orders into one entry per day to reduce clutter, while itemized tracking records each order individually for more detail.

faq.question: Does the integration track sales tax automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map Shopify sales tax liabilities to the correct QuickBooks accounts during setup.

faq.question: How do I track Shopify processing fees?
faq.answer: Assign a dedicated expense category during mapping so processing fees are automatically recorded and isolated.

faq.question: How do I verify Shopify payouts match my bank deposits?
faq.answer: Use the automated banking feed tab to match incoming deposits against Shopify clearing registries.
